title: Buffer Overflow, Underflow, and Copy Boundaries · C Memory Workbench
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working bounded frame transformation while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded frame transformation while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive half-open ranges and prove start, count, end, and terminator relationships for changed empty and maximum cases.
Validate both readable source and writable destination extents before memcpy-like operations.
Choose memcpy only for proven non-overlap and memmove for overlapping byte ranges.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Off By One Proof
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 3994. Produce bounded C evidence to move overlapping bytes inside proven bounds; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive half-open ranges and prove start, count, end, and terminator relationships for changed empty and maximum cases.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Fortify Runtime Limits, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 4610. Produce bounded C evidence to move overlapping bytes inside proven bounds; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat fortified libraries and object-size checks as defense layers with optimization and visibility limits.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Fortify Runtime Limits, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: A fortified build makes all dynamic buffer operations memory safe.

stimulus.title: Fortify Runtime Limits failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A fortified build makes all dynamic buffer operations memory safe.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat fortified libraries and object-size checks as defense layers with optimization and visibility limits.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Fortify Runtime Limits case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Fortify Runtime Limits condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Fortify Runtime Limits
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Fortify Runtime Limits: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 2991. Produce bounded C evidence to move overlapping bytes inside proven bounds; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat fortified libraries and object-size checks as defense layers with optimization and visibility limits.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Fortify Runtime Limits.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Fortify Runtime Limits, keep Fortify Runtime Limits connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Treat fortified libraries and object-size checks as defense layers with optimization and visibility limits.
